steps.title: Factor out the common term
steps.content:
Both terms share a factor of $4t^2s$. Dividing $72t^2s^3$ by $4t^2s$ leaves $18s^2$, and dividing $60t^3s$ by $4t^2s$ leaves $15t$. Factor the common term out of the expression:
$$72t^2s^3-60t^3s=4t^2s(18s^2)-4t^2s(15t)$$

steps.title: Write the factored form
steps.content:
Combine the remaining terms inside one set of parentheses, preserving the subtraction between the original terms and keeping the shared factor in front:
$$4t^2s(18s^2-15t)$$
The answer is D.
